_meta.unit: Other-houses queue, house 6 (1st) - Bottega Veneta lexicon: models (with designer era) + materials/signature-techniques (Intrecciato DNA) + named colors (official vs descriptive)

types.model: bag model; the house-assigned name is the canonical key (Regime A). Designer era is load-bearing (Maier 2001-2018, Lee 2018-2021, Blazy 2021-2024, Trotter 2025-)
types.material: signature technique / leather / finish - Intrecciato is the house DNA; Maxi Intrecciato + Padded Intreccio are Lee-era variants
types.color: named color. KEY: unlike Dior/Gucci, BV genuinely NAMES its colors, so this is a real Hermes-like named-color layer. official_or_descriptive marks each: 'official' = confirmed BV house name (Parakeet); 'descriptive' = reseller-attributed (Fashionphile catalog name), very likely BV's own but not confirmed vs bottegaveneta.com this run

_meta.caveat: Regime A house. Model/technique/color kept as separate facts. BV NAMES its colors (the counter-case to Dior/Gucci who do not) - this is why BV has a real named-color archive. Only Parakeet is logged 'official' (double-sourced, SS2021); all other colors are 'descriptive (reseller-attributed)' at medium confidence, queued for promotion via bottegaveneta.com (Chrome path). bottegaveneta.com NOT scraped (Akamai bot-shell risk per LV/Dior/Gucci). No auction-grade BV page surfaced this run; sources were Vogue (models) + Fashionphile (colors) + SACLAB (history/Intrecciato origin). Loop/Mount/Point/Sunshine briefed but NOT sourced - logged honestly as unsourced, not invented. Cabat debut-year conflict (Vogue 2001 / SACLAB 2002) flagged not smoothed. Live cultural fact: Blazy left for Chanel (Dec 2024); Louise Trotter (ex-Carven) named CD, joined end Jan 2025 (official Kering announcement).

coverage.models: 17 sourced model rows (+5 briefed-but-unsourced logged honestly, not counted)

name: Cabat
note: The original Intrecciato statement tote: unlined, open-top, woven leather and nothing else, 'number plate' in detachable inner pouch; ~two days to craft, limited annual production. DEBUT-YEAR CONFLICT: Vogue says 2001 (Maier's first BV bag), SACLAB says 2002 - flagged not smoothed. Maxi Cabat is the Lee reissue.

name: Knot
note: Icon evening clutch: hinged minaudiere crowned by a signature looped-knot closure, Intrecciato clad in luxe fabrics; inspired by a '70s archive box clutch. BV held a dedicated Knot retrospective in St Tropez in 2004.

name: Veneta
note: The seamless rounded hobo: one continuous Intrecciato curve that loops over the shoulder, zip closure, fully lined; the quiet-luxury workhorse of the Maier years. Debut year not sourced this run (Maier era, 2000s).

name: Roma
note: Structured Intrecciato top-handle/tote of the Maier era; a more formal house shape. Roma Mini Intrecciato seen in SACLAB inventory; soldattire lists Roma among Maier-era defining pieces. Debut year not sourced.

name: Pouch
maps_to: Pouch / Mini Pouch / Chain Pouch / Intrecciato Pouch
note: Lee's first BV bag: supple calfskin folded and gathered like a dumpling, clam-like magnetic top; debuted SS2020 (named most-wanted product of 2019). Now mini/chain/intrecciato variants.

name: Jodie
note: The knotted-handle hobo: born unnamed, renamed after a paparazzi shot of Jodie Foster shielding herself with a large black BV bag. Lee put a knot in the classic Hobo handle; in stores early 2020 (Resort 2020). Comes Maxi/standard/Mini, always Intrecciato (except shearling).

name: Cassette
note: Small crossbody in the oversized Maxi Intrecciato weave ('a microscope view' of the classic weave); debuted pre-fall 2019. The Padded Cassette (FW2019) is the cult puffy version; later remixed with a gold chain strap.

name: Arco
note: Structured Intrecciato top-handle tote (triangle silhouette); a Lee-era day tote, also seen in shearling/Teddy lamb fur. Arco Tote Medium in SACLAB inventory. Debut ~2019-2020 (not precisely sourced this run).

name: Sardine
note: The white-hot Blazy It-bag: curved like the Jodie but with a rigid rod handle shaped like a gilt sardine fish; woven lambskin body. Debuted FW2022, Blazy's debut collection. Double-confirmed by luisaviaroma (Blazy debut It-bag).

name: Kalimero
note: Cylindrical bucket bag that OPENED Blazy's debut show in oxblood; offered in three iterations (loop to clutch/shoulder, bucket-strap, mini). Debuted FW2022 (Blazy's debut collection).

name: Clicker
note: Padded (not puffy) lambskin intreccio body on a shoulder strap of curved brass links recalling Elsa Peretti's nature-inspired jewelry; a Blazy It-bag. Debuted alongside the resort 2023 collection.

name: Andiamo
note: Structured-but-rounded calf Intrecciato bag with brass bow/knot top hardware ('andiamo' = 'let's go'); debuted SS2023. Three sizes, earth tones (inky green, chalk white, full-bodied reds). Fashionphile catalogs a Small Andiamo. A Large 'Baseball Andiamo' VIC-only limited edition also exists (IG lead, not logged as a separate model).

name: Hop
note: Angular 3D-Intreccio hobo, slung over the shoulder or arm; inspired by a BV hobo created in 2002 and 'very much giving Jodie'. Debuted on the FW2023 runway. Also a suede version.

name: Parachute
note: Re-released roomy tote in handwoven Intrecciato, updated with BV's seasonal colors and the Andiamo-style metallic knot detail. Two sizes; the smaller has a detachable crossbody strap. Re-released pre-fall 2024.

name: Lauren 1980
note: A Pouch-lineage clutch named in homage to Lauren Hutton, who carried a BV Pouch in 'American Gigolo' (referenced on the spring 2017 runway). The '1980' in the name nods to the film's year. Exact debut not sourced; Blazy-era.

name: Liberta
note: The maison's smooth-leather flap crossbody and the ONE current bag NOT offered in Intrecciato; braided-rope-and-U-shaped-hardware closure. Debuted on the FW2024 runway in soft/powder colors; two sizes.

name: Olimpia
note: Small structured Intrecciato crossbody/shoulder bag; a Lee/Blazy-era day shape. Olimpia Lamb in SACLAB inventory. Debut/era not precisely sourced; logged low.

name: Campana
note: LEAD ONLY: a 'Small Campana Bag' surfaced in a TikTok caption this run as a current BV model (offered in Pinecone and Dark Barolo). Confirms it's a real current model name, but no debut/era/identity source. Logged as a model-name lead, no year. NOT invented - caption-attested.

name: Intrecciato
note: THE house DNA: signature woven leather, hand-plaiting long strips of double-faced leather into a grid. ORIGIN (sourced, not folklore): Veneto ateliers' sewing machines could only handle thin leathers/fabric, so BV wove thin strips into something both beautiful and structurally strong. Technique developed in the 1960s (BV founded 1966); Fashionphile dates the weave to 'the 1960s'. Trained at BV's Scuola dei Maestri Pellettieri. The logo-less house signature ('When your own initials are enough').

name: Maxi Intrecciato / Maxi Intreccio
maps_to: Cassette, Maxi Cabat, Lee-era Pouch/Jodie variants
note: Daniel Lee's oversized blow-up of the classic Intrecciato weave: the same plait at a much larger scale ('a microscope view'). The surface of the Cassette, Maxi Cabat, and Lee-era variants. ~2018-2019.

name: Padded Intreccio / Nappa Maxi Intreccio Padded
note: The puffy, padded version of the Maxi weave; the fashion-crowd favourite finish. Debuted FW2019 with the Padded Cassette.

name: Nappa
note: Soft, smooth full-grain nappa leather; the base hide BV weaves into many Intrecciato bags (Nappa Intrecciato, Nappa Maxi Intreccio).

name: Butter Calfskin
note: BV's smooth, supple 'Butter' calfskin finish; a distinct hand from Nappa, named in resale cataloging (Butter Calfskin The Mini Pouch / Small Shoulder Pouch).

name: Lambskin
note: Soft lambskin used for the woven body of the Sardine and Padded Cassette variants (Lambskin Maxi Intrecciato).

name: Grained / Brushed / Patent calfskin
note: Textured and finished calfskin variants (grained, brushed, patent nappa) that change how a BV color reads on the same weave.

name: Shearling / Teddy
note: Shearling (and 'Teddy' lamb-fur) versions of the Jodie/Arco for FW seasons. Vogue notes the Jodie in shearling; SACLAB lists an Arco Tote in lamb-fur Teddy Gold.

name: Crocodile (incl. Himalaya)
note: Rare exotic-skin BV pieces (e.g. a Double Chain in Crocodile Himalaya); the top of the BV craft range.

name: Parakeet
note: BV's signature grassy/acid green, reintroduced at the SS2021 show, now synonymous with the brand and built into its store design/branding (the 'Hermes orange / Tiffany blue' of BV). Officially named Parakeet (sometimes called 'Grass'). Cross-ref: Vogue (via gucci.md) dates Parakeet Green to Lee 2021 and notes it being 'subtly fazed out' by 2023. The flagship OFFICIAL BV color.

name: Porridge
note: Ivory beige. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile); very likely BV's own name. A live trend lead (worklist TikTok read: 'Maxi Jodie in porridge') that checked out as a real BV color name. Year null - promote vs bottegaveneta.com.

name: Almond
note: Creamy beige. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile).

name: Dark Praline
note: Warm beige. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile).

name: Teak
note: Light brown. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile).

note: Classic tan. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile).

name: Fondant
note: Deep warm chocolate. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile); a live trend lead (worklist: 'Sardine in fondant') that checked out. Also in active Reddit r/handbags 'Fondant or Barolo' discourse. Year null - promote vs bottegaveneta.com.

name: Pinecone
note: Muted earthy taupe. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile); also caption-attested current (TikTok: Campana in Pinecone).

name: Plaster
note: Off-white. Reseller-attributed (Fashionphile).
